violations.text: Wood is used in the construction of platform and shelving in the (back storage) area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the beverage storage shelves in walk in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, and black residue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The ceiling and walls in the walk in cooler have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and black residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service and ice voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

Deli meat and cheese was observed held at (49-54) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

The presence of (rodents) is not controlled as evidenced by (droppings). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

Inspector observed a cat along with feeding equipment within the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies.PIC informed to remove cat and feeding equipment from establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
Containers of beverages were observed stored on the floor throughout the establishment. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (on a box with plastic ice bags) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

Walk in cooler located in the retail area was not in good repair. Ambient temperatures observed at 45DF.Estblishment not allowed to store TCS foods in unit until repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the storage shelves in walk in cooler and back storage have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the back storage area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2024

The floors in walk in cooler and back storage area have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and/or rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
